subject: Public Health
title: Drink-Driving: A Preventable Tragedy
description: One-minute skill: Drink-Driving: A Preventable Tragedy.

hook.text: Alcohol is involved in 27% of all road traffic deaths worldwide—over 350,000 preventable deaths each year.

buildup.text: Even small amounts of alcohol impair reaction time, judgment, and coordination—making driving dangerous.

discovery.text: Countries with strict blood alcohol limits—0.05% or lower—and random breath testing have far fewer alcohol-related.

twist.text: Many countries have legal BAC limits but no enforcement—laws without enforcement change nothing.

climax.text: Combining low BAC limits, sobriety checkpoints, and swift penalties reduces drink-driving deaths by up to 25%.

punchline.text: Every drink-driving death is 100% preventable. The law just needs to be.

quiz.question: What percentage of road traffic deaths involve alcohol?
